title: Bug fixes (3) + portfolio attribution auto-integration

bugs_found: signal_engine: economic_surprise and momentum_breadth signals computed but had zero weight in composite (missing from config signal_weights and regime_signal_weights) — v9.1 integration was effectively broken
bubble_detector: get_bubble_signal_score had a discontinuity at CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD — partial zone maxed at -0.25 but main zone started at -0.5, creating a jump
portfolio_engine: analyze_portfolio missing performance attribution vs SPY and MCTR risk contributions despite services being available
observation.gap_identified: Portfolio analysis endpoint returned basic metrics but lacked Bloomberg PORT-style attribution (Brinson-Fachler) and risk budgeting (MCTR) that the attribution service already supported

bugs_fixed: Added economic_surprise (0.05) and momentum_breadth (0.06) weights to signal_weights config and all 3 regime weight profiles (Bull/Bear/Volatile), rebalancing existing weights proportionally
Fixed bubble signal score continuity: partial zone now ramps to -0.5 at threshold (was -0.25), ensuring continuous mapping
Wired Brinson-Fachler attribution + MCTR into analyze_portfolio for automatic Bloomberg PORT-style analytics
implementation.feature_built: Auto-compute Brinson-Fachler performance attribution vs SPY and Marginal Contribution to Risk (MCTR) in portfolio analyze endpoint — users now get allocation/selection/interaction effects and per-asset risk budgets automatically

limitations: Portfolio attribution depends on yfinance data fetch for SPY benchmark — if fetch fails, attribution is silently skipped
MCTR uses sample covariance from already-fetched data rather than denoised covariance
Attribution uses 1-month period only; could extend to 3mo/1y for richer analysis
assessment.self_critique: Should have also added economic_surprise and momentum_breadth to the stock-level signal (get_stock_signal), but market-level was the priority since those are macro signals. The attribution integration reuses already-fetched price data efficiently but could benefit from using the denoised covariance matrix for MCTR.

next_steps: Add denoised covariance (RMT) to MCTR computation in analyze_portfolio
Wire attribution auto-compute for multiple periods (1mo, 3mo, YTD)
Add liquidity-adjusted position sizing to portfolio optimizer
Integrate Google Trends fear/greed into macro risk dashboard endpoint
Frontend: display attribution waterfall chart and MCTR bar chart on portfolio page
